questions.question: What does form.markAllAsTouched() do and when should you call it?
options: It submits the form programmatically
It marks all controls as touched so validation errors become visible — useful when a user clicks Submit without touching any fields
It disables all form controls to prevent further editing
It resets all controls to their initial values

questions.question: A cross-field validator (passwordsMatch) should be applied where?
options: On the password FormControl
On the confirmPassword FormControl
On the FormGroup — it receives the group as the AbstractControl and can read both controls
In the onSubmit() method as a manual if-check

questions.question: What is the difference between form.value and form.getRawValue()?
options: They are identical — both return the same object
form.value excludes disabled controls; form.getRawValue() includes all controls regardless of state
form.getRawValue() converts all values to strings; form.value preserves types
form.value is synchronous; form.getRawValue() returns a Promise

questions.question: What Angular module must be imported for reactive forms to work in a standalone component?
options: FormsModule
CommonModule
ReactiveFormsModule
FormModule

questions.question: The nonNullable option on FormControl does what?
options: Adds a required validator that prevents null values
Ensures the control value is never null — the typed value is always the specified type, not T | null
Prevents the form from being submitted when the control is null
Disables the input when the value becomes null
